plain_english: This measures your health report card for blood sugar over the last 3 months. It's the most accurate way to see how your body manages sugar.
senior_tip: Keep a small log of what you eat. A short 15-minute walk after lunch and dinner helps natural sugar control.
embedding_text: HbA1c glycated hemoglobin blood sugar diabetes glucose control three-month average

plain_english: This shows your sugar level after your body has rested overnight without food.
senior_tip: Ensure you fast for at least 8 to 10 hours before this test for the most accurate reading.
embedding_text: fasting blood glucose sugar level diabetes energy baseline

plain_english: This shows how well your body processes sugar specifically from the meal you just ate.
senior_tip: Avoid very heavy desserts or white bread right before this test to see your body's true performance.
embedding_text: post prandial blood sugar glucose after meal processing energy

plain_english: This is a measure of the total fatty substances in your blood that can affect heart health.
senior_tip: Small changes like using olive oil and eating more fiber can help keep this in check.
embedding_text: total cholesterol heart health fats lipids blood flow

plain_english: Often called 'bad' cholesterol, this can build up in your blood vessels if it gets too high.
senior_tip: Think of LDL as traffic in your pipes. Increasing green leafy vegetables acts like a cleaning crew.
embedding_text: low density lipoprotein ldl bad cholesterol arteries heart risk

plain_english: This is 'good' cholesterol. It acts like a broom, sweeping the bad cholesterol out of your system.
senior_tip: Regular gentle exercise and healthy fats like walnuts can help raise this 'good' protector.
embedding_text: high density lipoprotein hdl good cholesterol protection heart cleanup

plain_english: These are a type of fat found in your blood that your body uses for energy.
senior_tip: Limiting sugary drinks and alcohol is the fastest way to improve these numbers.
embedding_text: triglycerides fats energy storage blood health

plain_english: This measures the strength of your blood to carry oxygen. Low levels can make you feel tired or weak.
senior_tip: Include iron-rich foods like spinach, apples, or pomegranates to keep your energy levels high.
embedding_text: hemoglobin iron oxygen energy anemia blood count

plain_english: This hormone manages your body's engine speed. It affects energy, weight, and temperature.
senior_tip: If you feel unusually cold or tired all the time, your thyroid might be running a bit slow.
embedding_text: tsh thyroid metabolism energy weight control

plain_english: This shows how well your kidneys are filtering out waste. Think of kidneys as your body's water filter.
senior_tip: Staying well-hydrated is the best way to help your kidneys do their job smoothly.
embedding_text: creatinine kidney function filtration waste removal

plain_english: Urea is another waste product filtered by the kidneys. High levels might mean your body is slightly dehydrated.
senior_tip: Sipping warm water or herbal teas throughout the day keeps your system flushed and healthy.
embedding_text: blood urea nitrogen bun kidneys waste concentration

plain_english: This is an enzyme that helps your liver process food. Higher levels mean your liver might be working too hard.
senior_tip: Avoid unnecessary over-the-counter painkillers like paracetamol if your liver is stressed.
embedding_text: alt sgpt liver health enzyme metabolism

plain_english: Another liver enzyme. When elevated, it signals that the liver needs some extra care and rest.
senior_tip: Fresh home-cooked meals with less oil are the best gift you can give your liver.
embedding_text: ast sgot liver enzyme stress recovery

plain_english: This relates to how your liver handles old blood cells. High levels can cause a yellowish tint in the eyes (Jaundice).
senior_tip: A simple diet and plenty of rest are key if this marker is high.
embedding_text: bilirubin jaundice liver bile processing

plain_english: Crucial for bone strength and immunity. Low levels are very common in seniors.
senior_tip: 15 minutes of early morning sun and a few walnuts or fortified milk can make a huge difference.
embedding_text: vitamin d bones immunity sun vitamin

plain_english: Important for nerve health and memory. A common reason for feeling forgetful or having tingling in feet.
senior_tip: If you are vegetarian, you might need a small supplement as B12 is mostly found in dairy and meat.
embedding_text: vitamin b12 nerve health memory energy

plain_english: These are your body's soldiers. If the count is high, your body is likely fighting a small infection or inflammation.
senior_tip: If high, check for any symptoms like low-grade fever or aches and report to your doctor.
embedding_text: wbc white blood cells infection defense immunity

plain_english: These cells carry oxygen to every part of your body. Think of them as delivery trucks for energy.
senior_tip: Keep your breathing exercises regular to help these cells move oxygen more efficiently.
embedding_text: rbc red blood cells oxygen delivery energy

plain_english: These help your blood clot when you have a small cut. They prevent excessive bleeding.
senior_tip: Certain viral fevers can lower these. Keep an eye out for any easy bruising on your skin.
embedding_text: platelets clotting blood density healing

plain_english: This is the most accurate score of how well your kidneys are filtering. Higher is better.
senior_tip: Reducing salt in your food is the kindest thing you can do for your eGFR score.
embedding_text: egfr glomerular filtration kidney efficiency

plain_english: Helps manage water balance in your body. Low levels can cause confusion or feeling off-balance in seniors.
senior_tip: Avoid drinking excessive plain water too quickly; sipping is better for your salt balance.
embedding_text: sodium salt balance water retention hydration

plain_english: Crucial for heart rhythm and muscle function. Even small changes need attention.
senior_tip: Bananas and coconut water are natural sources, but check with your doctor before increasing them if your level is already high.
embedding_text: potassium heart rhythm muscles electrolytes

plain_english: High levels can settle in your joints and cause pain, often starting in the big toe (Gout).
senior_tip: Limit red meat and shellfish, and drink more water to help flush this crystal-forming acid out.
embedding_text: uric acid joint pain gout crystals

plain_english: Essential for bone strength and heart electrical signals.
senior_tip: Dairy, almonds, and small fish are great natural ways to support your bone health.
embedding_text: calcium bones heart rhythm minerals
